content.problemStatement:
Your teacher gave you an assignment — given an integer NNN, construct a binary string B=b1b2b3…bNB = b_1b_2b_3\ldots b_NB=b1​b2​b3​…bN​ of length NNN such that:

max⁡(bi,bi+1)=1\max(b_i, b_{i+1}) = 1max(bi​,bi+1​)=1for **every** iii from 111 to N−1N-1N−1.

What is the **minimum number** of 111's such a binary string can contain?

**Note:** A binary string is a string consisting of only the digits 000 and 111.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case contains a single integer NNN — the length of binary string you'd like to construct.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of 111's required to complete the assignment.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (2 ≤ N ≤ 1000) – the required length of the binary string.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the minimum possible number of '1' characters in any binary string of length N that satisfies max(b_i, b_{i+1}) = 1 for all 1 ≤ i < N.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each N the answer is floor(N/2): 6→3, 8→4, 2→1, 3→1, 5→2, 100→50.

solution.approach: The condition max(b_i, b_{i+1}) = 1 forbids two consecutive zeros, i.e., the string must not contain the substring "00". To minimise the number of ones we want to place as many zeros as possible while respecting this rule. The optimal pattern is 010101… (starting with 0). In this pattern zeros appear at every other position, giving the maximum possible number of zeros = ceil(N/2). Consequently the minimum number of ones is N - ceil(N/2) = floor(N/2). Therefore for each N the answer is simply N // 2 (integer division). The algorithm runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        out.append(str(N // 2))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
